docs.text: Welcome to Active Global Protection theme by Coalition Technologies documentation Developers info Parent theme: Blocksy 2.1.49 Child theme: blocksy-child (Active Global Protection - Coalition Technologies Theme) Greenshift The blocks on this website were created with the Greenshift block-builder plugin. For setup, editing, and full documentation, see the Greenshift documentation: Greenshift Learning Center Block gallery Custom Sections and Pages Menu Form Wp Blocks Blocksy Theme Customizer Guide The Blocksy theme provides a user-friendly WordPress Theme Customizer , allowing you to adjust the appearance and layout of your site in real time. This guide walks through all the main options and best practices. Accessing the Theme Customizer Log in to your WordPress dashboard. Navigate to Appearance → Customize . The Customizer panel will open on the left, with a live preview on the right. Note: Changes are not visible on your site until you click Publish . Customizer Sections Here we have all the theme settings Site Identity Site Title: Update your site’s name. Tagline: Add a short description for your site. Logo: Upload your logo image (recommended size: 200x60px). Site Icon / Favicon: Upload a 512x512px icon that appears in browser tabs. Colors & Typography Primary & Secondary Colors: Customize theme accent colors. Background Color: Set the site background color. Font Family & Size: Choose fonts for headings, body, and menus. Link Colors: Change link hover and active colors. Header Options Header Settings: Align menus and adjust spacing. You can change the setting by clicking on the COG icon Sticky Header: Enable a header that stays visible on scroll. Article: Header builder elements Blog / Archive Layout Options: Grid, list, or full-width blog layouts. Excerpt Length: Control the number of words displayed per post. Featured Image Display: Show or hide thumbnails on the blog page. Footer Options Footer Layout: One, two, or three-column layout. Copyright Text: Customize the copyright message. Footer Widgets: Enable or disable widget areas.
docs.title: Theme Settings

docs.text: WP Blocks Section To add green text on the section: You can directly change the text in the Block editor by clicking on the text, and then clicking save. Copying Blocks Follow these steps to copy a block in the page builder: Select the block column Click on the section you want to copy, click on the three dots, and then select 'Copy'. Copy the block Click the three dots on the block toolbar and select Copy . Also, you can copy the block from side bar panel Click on the three dots and click on the 'Copy' Paste the block on a new page Open the page where you want to paste the block. Press: Command + V on Mac Ctrl + V on Windows Also, you will see pink and blue blocks in the sidebar. Both can be copied and used on other pages, but they behave differently when edited: Pink blocks: Editing a pink block will reflect changes across all pages where that template is used. Blue blocks: Editing a blue block will only affect the current page. To use a reusable template, click on the 'Add Block' button and select 'Reusable Templates'. Reusable Templates (Synced Pattern) Follow these steps to update a reusable templates: Those are the global sections that are used in multiple pages. If you change it here, it will change in all the pages where it is used. Go to Reusable Templates Click on the Reusable Templates in the left sidebar. Edit the reusable template Click on 'Edit' button of the reusable template you want to update. Click on the text you want to update. Click on the 'Save' button to save the changes. Useful Article Please refer to these articles for more information: Blocksy Documentation General Options
docs.title: WP Blocks

Also, you can copy the block from side bar panel Click on the three dots and click on the 'Copy' Paste the block on a new page Open the page where you want to paste the block. Press: Command + V on Mac Ctrl + V on Windows Also, you will see pink and blue blocks in the sidebar. Both can be copied and used on other pages, but they behave differently when edited: Pink blocks: Editing a pink block will reflect changes across all pages where that template is used. docs.text: Follow these steps to update a reusable templates: Those are the global sections that are used in multiple pages. If you change it here, it will change in all the pages where it is used. Go to Reusable Templates Click on the Reusable Templates in the left sidebar. Edit the reusable template Click on 'Edit' button of the reusable template you want to update. Click on the text you want to update. Click on the 'Save' button to save the changes.

docs.text: Forms — Create, Add in pages 1. Create a Form WP admin → Fluent Forms → New Form. Give it a title and description. Add fields (drag & drop) and configure field settings. Configure Confirmations: Fluent Forms → your form → Settings → Confirmations. Save the form and note the form ID (shown on the forms list or the form editor). 2. Add Form to a page Create a dedicated WP page that contains only the form (use the shortcode: [fluentform id="1"]).
docs.title: Form

docs.text: WP admin → Fluent Forms → New Form. Give it a title and description. Add fields (drag & drop) and configure field settings. Configure Confirmations: Fluent Forms → your form → Settings → Confirmations. Save the form and note the form ID (shown on the forms list or the form editor).
docs.title: 1. Create a Form

docs.text: Create a dedicated WP page that contains only the form (use the shortcode: [fluentform id="1"]).
docs.title: 2. Add Form to a page

docs.text: Menu WP Admin → Appearance → Menus. Create/choose a menu, add Pages, Custom Links, Categories. Arrange by drag & drop; set Display Location; Save.
docs.title: Menu
